description:
# Inline closures whose function identity and captures are statically known

## Goal

Extend `ir_reducer` so that when a Var has Descr
`closure_lit(F, [capture_descrs])` and the captures reduce to
literals (or to other closure_lits whose captures are literal,
transitively), a call through that closure inlines F's body with
the captures substituted.

Mixed cases (some captures literal, some opaque) reduce partially:
the literal captures dissolve into substituted constants; the
opaque ones remain as parameters.

## Acceptance

- `closure_typed_captures` fixture: full reduction → `print(42)`.
  Zero closure heap objects allocated at runtime.
- `curried_add` fixture: full reduction → `print(42); print(6)`.
- `spawn2_basic` fixture: lambda body reduces; spawn target is the
  literal `send(1, 42); halt` thunk. Zero `child` body.
- `spawn_with_captures` fixture (when called with literal arg):
  closure dissolves to a static thunk with no heap allocation.
- `higher_order`: full reduction of `apply2`, `compose`.

## Removals

None yet.
title: fz-RED.5: closure_lit reduction
